hints: Use re.compile(r"^\d{5}(?:-\d{4})?$") as the pattern. \d{5} means exactly five digits; (?:-\d{4})? makes the hyphen-plus-four-digits part optional.
re.fullmatch checks the entire string against the pattern — nothing can appear before or after. It's equivalent to re.search with ^ and $ anchors.
Return bool(ZIP_PATTERN.fullmatch(code)) to get True or False. A match object is truthy, but the tests check isinstance(result, bool), so you need the explicit conversion.

The non-capturing group (?:-\d{4}) groups the hyphen and four digits together so the ? applies to both at once. Without the group, the ? would only make the last \d{4} optional, leaving a dangling hyphen.
